CREATE TABLE test_config (id INT PRIMARY KEY, http_enable_gz INT);
SELECT http_enable_gz FROM test_config WHERE id = 1;
SELECT http_enable_gz FROM test_config WHERE id = 2;
UPDATE test_config SET http_enable_gz = 1 WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT http_enable_gz FROM test_config WHERE id = 2) AS subquery;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_config WHERE http_enable_gz = 1 AND id IN (SELECT id FROM test_config WHERE id < 3);
SELECT id, http_enable_gz FROM test_config WHERE http_enable_gz = (SELECT MAX(http_enable_gz) FROM test_config);
DELETE FROM test_config WHERE http_enable_gz = 0 AND id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM test_config WHERE http_enable_gz = 0);
CREATE TABLE additional_settings (setting_id INT PRIMARY KEY, setting_value INT);
SELECT t.id, t.http_enable_gz, a.setting_value FROM test_config t JOIN additional_settings a ON t.id = a.setting_id WHERE a.setting_value = 1;
UPDATE test_config SET http_enable_gz = (SELECT MAX(setting_value) FROM additional_settings) WHERE id = 1;
SELECT t.id, t.http_enable_gz FROM test_config t WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM additional_settings a WHERE a.setting_value = t.http_enable_gz);
WITH ConfigCTE AS (SELECT http_enable_gz FROM test_config WHERE id = 1) SELECT * FROM ConfigCTE;
